title: Living neurons enter the data center as governance lags - TCR 04/29/26
description:
Cortical Labs opened the world's first biological data center in Melbourne with 200,000 lab-grown human neurons per CL1 unit, EnerVenue abandoned its $264M Kentucky nickel-hydrogen battery factory to build in Changzhou, and 700 Meta AI safety trainers at Covalen Dublin faced layoffs with a six-month non-compete locking them out of competing Meta vendors.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-29-2026/

0:00 EnerVenue pivots battery factory to Changzhou
1:00 Cortical Labs opens first biological data center
4:00 Tacit Knowledge Has A Zip Code
11:38 Scout AI and the Vision Language Action frontier
14:14 What shipped

This episode covers Cortical Labs' biological computing rollout, EnerVenue's pivot from Kentucky to Changzhou, the Covalen layoffs and the proprietary architecture of AI training labor, the FCC's accelerated review of Disney broadcast licenses, Scout AI's $100M raise for military Vision Language Action models, and China's nationwide robotaxi license freeze after the Baidu Wuhan incident.

A data center where the processors are alive. A battery factory moving to where the manufacturing knowledge lives. A non-compete clause for workers training their replacements. A regulator reaching for a 1934 lever. A defense contract writing its own rules of engagement. Cortical Labs' CL1 systems run 200,000 lab-grown human neurons each on silicon microelectrode arrays, with a Singapore facility already planned - the first commercial deployment of biological computing infrastructure. EnerVenue, whose nickel-hydrogen chemistry powered the ISS and Hubble and lasts 30,000 cycles, abandoned $264M in Kentucky to build in Changzhou where the engineers who know how to commission first-of-a-kind battery factories actually live. More than 700 Covalen workers in Dublin who red-team Meta's AI safety systems learned their jobs are at risk - and a six-month cooldown clause prevents them from joining competing Meta vendors, structuring the entire labor market as a proprietary supply chain. The FCC ordered Disney to file ABC broadcast renewals years ahead of schedule, applying pressure to the part of the speech ecosystem that has lost its scale. Scout AI raised $100M to train Vision Language Action models on autonomous all-terrain vehicles at a California military base, with the CTO describing the goal as "an incredible military AGI." China froze new robotaxi licenses nationwide after Baidu Apollo Go vehicles stalled Wuhan traffic. The pattern across every story: institutions are operating in domains their formal mandates barely cover, and the gap is being filled by private contracts.
